behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary: source collectors + aggregation + summary-input builder. A unit test of one function can miss wiring/field-drop bugs between modules (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network/subprocess is not required to catch this specific behavior; in-process fakes for the three source clients are sufficient (REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). Correctness here is about payload completeness, not model output quality (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level System because behavior text only requires that content "passed to the summary-generation step" contains all sources, not that real endpoints are contacted.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline up to (but not including) summary generation with stubbed source clients returning distinct fixtures; assert the exact payload handed to the summary step includes metrics, issues, and deployment sections from all three sources.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback formatting (collector failure -> report assembly), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). The unavailability condition can be reproduced by a failing stub client/raised timeout exception; no real network outage is required for this behavior (REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). It does not depend on LLM judgment quality (b=no) and is not multi-agentic (c=no). Rejected System because behavior text focuses on graceful degradation output, not real transport-layer failure characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Stub two sources as successful and one source as unavailable (exception/error response), execute report generation, and assert process completes and output contains a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about semantic fidelity of generated narrative to input values, which depends on actual model output quality; deterministic stubs can always pass and hide real misrepresentation (LLM-DEP yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). This is not just routing/retry infrastructure. Rejected Workflow because behavior text specifies one summary-generation outcome, not multiple sequential agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary generation against the real model using representative weekly datasets and assert key numeric/boolean facts are preserved in the produced narrative (with tolerance rubric for wording variance but strict fact consistency).

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary includes real external email delivery and inbox receipt, which requires real HTTP/SMTP/service behavior outside process fakes (REAL-INFRA yes). Mocked delivery can hide auth, provider acceptance, and delivery-path issues (MOCK-HIDE yes). Agentic quality is not the asserted dimension here; behavior text emphasizes end-to-end reachability and correct recipient inbox delivery. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no requirement in this behavior text to validate model decision quality or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end scheduled run in a test environment with real source endpoints (or production-like test endpoints) and real email service credentials; verify the configured recipient mailbox receives the report with expected subject/recipient metadata.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is orchestration/state handoff: delivery failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry consumption. This spans modules and persistence, so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). Can be tested with in-process failing email client and real local temp filesystem across two invoked runs; no real external email outage or OS scheduler process is strictly required (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No dependence on model output quality (b=no) and no multi-agentic chain (c=no). Rejected System because behavior text requires retry on "next scheduled run" semantics, which can be reproduced by invoking scheduler logic twice in test without real cron/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: First run: force email client unreachable, assert report file is written to local fallback path. Second run: restore email client, execute scheduler/run loop again, assert persisted report is retried and sent, and fallback artifact state is updated/cleared per design.
